sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa dell'aumento del VIX del 13.16% a 31.05, indicando un aumento dell'avversione al rischio. Il DXY è salito dello 0.25% a 100.15, mentre l'EUR/USD è sceso dello 0.23% a 1.15. Il prezzo del petrolio è sceso del 2.49% a $105.32, ma rimane elevato. La chiusura dei mercati tradizionali per il fine settimana aumenta l'incertezza.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to the 13.16% increase in VIX to 31.05, indicating rising risk aversion. DXY rose 0.25% to 100.15, while EUR/USD fell 0.23% to 1.15. Oil price dropped 2.49% to $105.32 but remains high. The closure of traditional markets for the weekend adds to uncertainty.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario S&P 500 ha chiuso in calo del 1.67% a 6368.85. Il TLT Bond USA 20Y è sceso dello 0.55% a 85.64. L'oro è salito del 3.40% a $4524.30/oz. Il Bitcoin è salito dello 0.47% a $66,634. Il Fear & Greed Index è a 9, indicando un'estrema paura.
market_impact_summary.en: The S&P 500 stock market closed down 1.67% at 6368.85. The TLT Bond USA 20Y fell 0.55% to 85.64. Gold rose 3.40% to $4524.30/oz. Bitcoin increased 0.47% to $66,634. The Fear & Greed Index is at 9, indicating extreme fear.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno a tutti, benvenuti al nostro briefing finanziario di oggi, sabato 29 marzo 2026.

Mentre le borse mondiali osservano la consueta pausa festiva del fine settimana, il sentiment globale risulta essere prudente, con una propensione verso asset più sicuri. La chiusura dei mercati asiatici ha visto il Nikkei giapponese scendere dello 0,43%, mentre lo Shanghai cinese ha guadagnato lo 0,63%.

Nella chiusura precedente, l'S&P 500 ha registrato una discesa significativa del 1,67%, chiudendo a 6368,85. Il Dollar Index (DXY) ha guadagnato lo 0,25%, attestandosi a 100,15. Il VIX, indice della volatilità, è salito del 13,16% a 31,05. L'oro ha brillato, guadagnando il 3,40% a 4524,30 dollari l'oncia. Il petrolio Brent è sceso del 2,49% a 105,32 dollari. I tassi di interesse continuano a essere oggetto di attenzione, con il tasso decennale USA che è salito dello 0,54% a 4,44%.

Sul fronte geopolitico, crescono le preoccupazioni riguardo a possibili escalation nel conflitto in Iran, con impatti sui mercati energetici e valutari. La BCE ha recentemente mantenuto i tassi di interesse al 2,00%.

Mentre i mercati tradizionali sono chiusi, il comparto digitale continua a operare 24 ore su 24. Vediamo come si stanno comportando gli asset digitali in questo momento di incertezza globale.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con una analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.

Bitcoin è sceso leggermente, portandosi a $66,632, con un incremento dello 0.34%. Tuttavia, gli ETF hanno registrato un deflusso di $-225.5M, segnale di una possibile debolezza nel breve termine.

Ethereum è stabile intorno ai $2,002, con un incremento del 0.02%. Solana è scesa dell'0.87% a $82.36, mentre Binance Coin (BNB) ha guadagnato lo 0.04% portandosi a $613.34. Queste mosse suggeriscono una certa stabilità nel mercato delle altcoin.

L'indice Fear & Greed è a 9, in territorio di Estrema Paura. Questo livello di paura potrebbe indicare una possibile inversione del trend nel breve termine. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici, come il recente tasso di inflazione e i dati sul mercato del lavoro, potrebbe influenzare ulteriormente il sentiment degli investitori.

In generale, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di attesa, con gli investitori che monitorano attentamente i dati macroeconomici e le notizie regolamentari. La mancanza di chiarezza sulle regole future per le criptovalute negli Stati Uniti, insieme alle preoccupazioni sulla sicurezza, continua a influenzare il mercato.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is risk-off, with investors digesting a mix of economic data and geopolitical tensions. Key data points include the US Core CPI Year over Year at 2.7%, and the recent PCE Core Year over Year at 3.1%.

Traditional markets are closed for the weekend. At the last close, equities were under pressure: the S&P 500 at 6368.85, down 1.67%, and the STOXX 600 at 57.05, down 0.90%. Bonds saw selling pressure, with the US 10Y Yield at 4.44%, up 0.54%, and the TLT Bond USA 20Y at 85.64, down 0.55%. In currencies, the Euro to US Dollar at 1.15, down 0.23%, and the Dollar Index at 100.15, up 0.25%. Commodities saw gold rally to $4524.30 per ounce, up 3.40%, while Brent crude fell to $105.32, down 2.49%.

Geopolitical tensions are on the rise, particularly with concerns over a potential escalation in Iran. This has led to increased borrowing costs in the Eurozone and impacted global markets. The economic architecture of the eurozone is being scrutinized, and there's a focus on energy security amid rising prices.

While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which trade around the clock. Let's turn our attention to the current state of cryptocurrencies.

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

Starting with Bitcoin, we see that the daily net inflow for BTC ETFs was $-225.5M, indicating a significant outflow. From a technical standpoint, BTC is trading at $66,632, up 0.34% for the day. 

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the top altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,002, up 0.02%, while Solana is at $82.36, down 0.87%. Binance Coin is trading at $613.34, up 0.04%. 

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 9, indicating extreme fear. This is also reflected in the market correlation, with many assets experiencing significant price movements. 

Overall, it's clear that the cryptocurrency market is experiencing a period of heightened volatility, with many traders and investors exercising caution. As always, we'll be keeping a close eye on these developments and bringing you updates as more information becomes available.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another update. Have a great day and we'll see you then. Goodbye.

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…
articles.title_it: La sicurezza economica è una sfida crescente per le economie mondiali
articles.title_en: Economic security is a growing challenge for global economies
articles.summary_it: La sicurezza economica è una sfida crescente per le economie mondiali a causa della crescente instabilità geopolitica.
articles.summary_en: Economic security is a growing challenge for global economies due to rising geopolitical instability.

articles.title: US Escalation Is the Most Likely Scenario in Iran
articles.snippet: If Donald Trump walks away from the war with Iran now, the threat to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz will remain, risk premia on oil prices will stay permanently higher, and Trump’s own popularity will sink even further ahead of this year’s midterm elections. Despite the obvious risks, he has every reason to try to “finish the job.”

articles.title: The future economic architecture of the eurozone
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics is pleased to host a high-level panel discussion on the future economic architecture of the eurozone, featuring Spanish finance minister Carlos Cuerpo Caballero and ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel, alongside leading experts Olivier Blanchard of PIIE and Ángel Ubide of Citadel. Following opening remarks by Cuerpo Caballero, the conversation explores how much monetary policy transmission and financial stability in the eurozone depend on deeper integration, the opportunities and challenges posed by rising long-term commitments to…

articles.title: Drillers See Triple-Digit Crude and Hit the Brakes
articles.snippet: Brent crude is trading over $100 per barrel, WTI has topped $90, but oil drillers in the world’s largest producer are cautious about their future plans. In fact, they are rather unhappy with the war in the Middle East, because it has made it harder to plan investments. On the face of it, everything is perfect, price-wise. WTI is trading much higher than what shale drillers need to be profitable. According to the latest Dallas Fed Energy Survey, the range of WTI profitable drilling price levels for the oil patch is between $62 per barrel for…

articles.title: Why Spain Is Weathering Europe’s Energy Crunch Better Than Most
articles.snippet: Europe has “sleepwalked into yet another energy crisis” watching energy prices spike across the continent for the second time in four years due to instability in global politics and energy trade. Four years after the Russian invasion of Ukraine sent global energy markets reeling, Europe has made significant progress weaning itself off of Russian oil and gas imports and building up its own energy supply chains. But the European Union still relies on imports for more than half of its energy needs, leaving it achingly vulnerable to the…

articles.title: Why the Iran Conflict Is Good News for Chinese Automakers
articles.snippet: A sharp rise in oil prices tied to the US-Israel confrontation with Iran is likely to speed up the global transition to electric vehicles, strengthening a shift that has already helped China overtake Japan as the world’s top car seller, according to South China Morning Post. Crude prices have surged past $100 a barrel amid fears of disruption to energy supplies, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump escalated tensions by warning he would “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if shipping through…

articles.title: Russia Warns of Force Majeure on Oil Cargoes After Port Disruptions
articles.snippet: Russia’s oil exporters are warning buyers that cargoes from its key Baltic ports may not be delivered at all. Russian producers are now saying they may declare force majeure on cargoes from key Baltic Sea ports after a sustained wave of Ukrainian drone strikes knocked out critical infrastructure this week. The epicenter is Ust-Luga, one of Russia’s most important export terminals, where oil loadings have been halted since Wednesday following repeated strikes from Ukraine and a fire that, as of Friday, was still burning. Industry sources…

articles.title: Middle East War Reignites the Energy Transition
articles.snippet: The worst oil and gas supply shock in history has exposed the vulnerability of dependence on fossil fuel imports and is making renewables popular again. As governments scramble to contain the fallout from the energy shock, both in supply and prices, increased electrification in transportation and power generation is once again the talk of the town. As the war in the Middle East laid bare the shock of losing oil and gas supply, policymakers and analysts are once again considering the benefits of fossil fuel importers boosting the share of renewable…
